target_audience.primary_persona: Solo travelers who plan and manage their own adventures independently
secondary_personas: Safety-conscious travelers who need check-in and location sharing features
Travel enthusiasts who want to document and share their journeys
Backpackers and budget travelers seeking destination recommendations
Female solo travelers prioritizing safety features
Digital nomads who need offline access and trip organization tools
pain_points: Planning trips alone without input from travel companions
Safety concerns when traveling solo (no one to check in on you)
Difficulty discovering solo-friendly destinations and accommodations
Lack of community and connection with fellow solo travelers
Managing complex itineraries and travel documents independently
Limited offline access to travel plans in areas with poor connectivity
Privacy concerns with location sharing and travel data
Difficulty organizing and sharing travel memories and experiences
goals: Stay safe while traveling alone with automated check-ins
Discover new destinations that are safe and suitable for solo travel
Connect with other solo travelers for tips and meetups
Document travel experiences with photos, notes, and journals
Access travel plans offline in remote areas
Share location with trusted contacts for safety
Find community and avoid feeling isolated while traveling alone
target_audience.usage_context: Used before, during, and after solo trips. Pre-trip for planning and booking, during travel for navigation, check-ins, journaling, and safety, and post-trip for memory preservation and sharing with community. Designed for both frequent solo travelers and occasional independent adventurers.

product_vision.one_liner: A comprehensive travel companion app designed specifically for solo travelers to plan adventures, stay safe, discover destinations, and connect with a community of fellow independent explorers.
product_vision.problem_statement: Solo travelers face unique challenges that traditional travel apps don't address: planning trips without companions, safety concerns when traveling alone, difficulty finding solo-friendly destinations, and lack of community connection with fellow independent travelers. Existing travel apps are designed for group travel or don't provide the safety and community features solo travelers need.
product_vision.value_proposition: SoloAdventurer is the only travel app built from the ground up for solo travelers, combining comprehensive trip planning, automated safety check-ins, solo-specific destination recommendations, offline-first architecture, and a supportive community of fellow solo adventurers. Unlike generic travel apps, we address the specific pain points of traveling alone while respecting privacy and enabling authentic connections.
success_metrics: User retention: 40% DAU/MAU ratio indicating daily engagement during trips
Trip completion rate: 80% of planned trips marked as completed
Safety feature usage: 60% of users actively using check-in features
Offline functionality: 90% of users successfully accessing plans without connectivity
Community engagement: 30% of users connecting with other travelers
App store rating: 4.5+ stars with positive safety feedback
Trip journaling: 50% of users creating at least one journal entry per trip
Session duration: Average 15+ minutes per session during trip planning and active travel

existing_features: AWS Cognito authentication with token refresh system
User profile management (domain and data layers complete)
Travel planning module with trip creation and management
Travel journal with rich text editing and media support
Safety check-in and location sharing features
Offline-first architecture with local data persistence
Background services for notifications and check-ins
Privacy-first safety features with granular controls
Performance optimization for large trip datasets
known_gaps: Profile feature presentation layer incomplete (domain and data done, UI pending)
Social features for connecting travelers not yet implemented
Public trip sharing and social journaling incomplete
AI-powered destination recommendations not yet integrated
Trip sharing functionality in progress (TODO comments in code)
Platform-specific sharing features not fully implemented
Advanced filtering and search capabilities for journals and trips
Collaborative trip planning for temporary groups
technical_debt: AWS CloudWatch monitoring packages temporarily disabled due to dependency conflicts (aws_cloudwatch, aws_cloudwatch_api commented out)
Multiple TODO comments indicating incomplete features (social sharing, journal editing, platform-specific availability checks)
Riverpod 3.0 migration recently completed - potential for migration-related issues or refactoring needs
Offline-Aware Repository registrations commented out in offline_module.dart
Some auth service provider overrides need proper implementation in tests
Cached data provider has incomplete trip caching implementation
Background check-in service needs integration with SafetyRepository
Notification payload navigation to appropriate screens incomplete
Journal search optimization needed for tag filtering performance
Timeline filtering implementation marked as TODO
Supabase integration for certain features may need alignment with AWS Cognito auth

alternatives: Generic travel planning apps (TripIt, Google Trips, Sygic Travel)
Social travel apps (Couchsurfing, TravelBFF, Travel Buddies)
Safety-focused apps (bSafe, Kitestring, Red Panic Button)
Travel journaling apps (Polarsteps, Travel Diaries, JRNY)
Destination discovery platforms (Lonely Planet, TripAdvisor, Culture Trip)
Backpacking and budget travel apps (Hostelworld, Rome2Rio, Wanderlog)
differentiators: Solo-travel-first approach - every feature designed specifically for independent travelers
Comprehensive safety suite with automated check-ins and granular privacy controls
Offline-first architecture enabling access in remote destinations without connectivity
Combination of planning, safety, journaling, and community in one platform
Privacy-first design giving users full control over location sharing and data
Clean architecture with robust sync and conflict resolution
Cross-platform mobile app (iOS and Android) with native performance
competitive_context.market_position: A niche player in the solo travel market targeting independent travelers aged 25-45 who value safety, organization, and community. Positioned between generic travel planning tools (which lack safety features) and safety-only apps (which lack comprehensive travel tools). Competes on depth of solo-specific features rather than breadth of general travel capabilities.

technical: Flutter/Dart cross-platform development limiting native platform-specific features
AWS Cognito dependency for authentication (vendor lock-in considerations)
Supabase integration alongside AWS services adds complexity to backend architecture
Offline-first architecture requires careful sync conflict resolution
Google Maps API usage limits and costs for mapping features
Mobile app store approval processes for safety and location features
Background service limitations on iOS due to operating system restrictions
Performance constraints on older mobile devices for large trip datasets
resources: Part-time development effort alongside other commitments
No dedicated QA or testing team - reliance on automated tests
Self-funded development with limited marketing budget
Open-source project relying on community contributions for some features
Time constraints due to solo development requiring prioritization of features
dependencies: AWS Cognito for user authentication and management
Supabase for certain backend services and database operations
Google Maps API for mapping and navigation features
App store and Play Store approval for distribution
Sentry for error and crash reporting
Device capabilities (GPS, camera, storage) for core features
Background task scheduling (workmanager) with OS limitations
Network connectivity for sync and API calls
